name: Railway Account API Token
description: Creates an account-level API token for Railway. This token provides access to all projects and services in your Railway account via the Railway GraphQL API.

steps.title: Open the Railway tokens page
steps.instruction: Sign in to your Railway account and navigate to Account Settings > Tokens.

steps.title: Click 'Create Token' or '+ New Token'
steps.instruction: Look for the button to create a new API token. It may be labelled 'Create Token', '+ New Token', or similar, depending on the current Railway UI.
steps.screenshot_hint: Button to create a new token, usually near the top of the tokens list

steps.title: Name the token
steps.instruction: Enter a descriptive name for the token so you can identify it later, for example 'rialu-poller' or 'deploy-automation'.

steps.title: Confirm creation
steps.instruction: Click the confirmation button to generate the token. Railway will display the token value on screen.
steps.screenshot_hint: Dialog or inline display showing the newly generated token string

steps.title: Copy the token
steps.instruction: Copy the token value immediately. Railway account tokens are typically long alphanumeric strings (UUID-like or base64-encoded).
steps.warning: Railway only displays the token once at creation. If you navigate away without copying it, you will need to delete the token and create a new one.

steps.title: Verify the token works
steps.instruction: Open a terminal and run: curl -s -X POST https://backboard.railway.com/graphql/v2 -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Authorization: Bearer <your-token>' -d '{"query":"{me{email}}"}' — you should see your email in the JSON response.
steps.screenshot_hint: Terminal showing a JSON response containing your Railway account email

vault_entry.notes: Account-level API token for Railway GraphQL API. Access to all projects and services.
